---
applyTo: "**/*.md"
---

# Markdown Standards

## General

- Use **ATX-style headings** (`#` prefix) — never underline-style (`===` / `---`)
- One blank line before and after every heading, code block, table, and list
- Wrap inline code, commands, package names, file paths, and property names in backticks
- Use bold (`**text**`) for key terms, labels, warnings, and inline section titles in README bodies
- No trailing spaces; end files with a single newline

## Headings

- `h1` (`#`) — document/package title only, one per file
- `h2` (`##`) — top-level sections
- `h3` (`###`) — sub-sections (e.g. Props, Events, Examples within component docs)
- Do not skip heading levels

## README Files

Standard structure for package READMEs:

```markdown
# @scope/package-name

One sentence description of what the package provides.

**Recommendations**
Any install caveats or guidance.

**Installing**
`pnpm add [-D] @scope/package-name`

**Setup**
...
```

- Use a bold label on its own line (e.g. `**Installing**`) as an inline section header when a full `##` heading would be too heavy
- Show install commands as inline code, not in fenced blocks
- Use fenced code blocks with a language tag for any multi-line example code

## CHANGELOG Files

Generated by [Changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets). Do not edit manually.

Expected format:

```markdown
# @scope/package-name

## 1.2.3

### Patch Changes

- abc1234: Description of the change

## 1.2.0

### Minor Changes

- def5678: Description of the change

### Patch Changes

- Updated dependencies [abc1234]
  - @scope/other-package@x.y.z
```

Change categories: `### Patch Changes`, `### Minor Changes`, `### Major Changes`.

## Component Documentation (`.md` files in `apps/building-blocks/docs/`)

These files are compiled into Vue components by `unplugin-vue-markdown`. They may contain `<script setup>` blocks to import Vue components for live examples.

### Props table

| Column | Content |
|---|---|
| Prop name | Exact prop name as declared |
| Description | Copied from JSDoc on the prop |
| Type | TypeScript type |
| Values | Allowed values or `-` |
| Default | Default value or blank |

Example:

```markdown
### Props

| Prop name | Description          | Type    | Values | Default |
| --------- | -------------------- | ------- | ------ | ------- |
| model     | The bound data model | IpModel | -      |         |
| readOnly  | Enables read-only    | boolean | -      | false   |
```

### Events table

| Column | Content |
|---|---|
| Event name | Exact emit name |
| Properties | Bold arg name + type + description |
| Description | What triggers / what the event communicates |

Example:

```markdown
### Events

| Event name | Properties                                  | Description                      |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| save       | **payload** `T` - A key-value pair of value | Emitted when saved successfully  |
```

### Examples section

```markdown
### Examples

```ts
import MyComponent from '@uis/uisvue/my-component';
```

#### General usage

<MyComponent />
<CodeBlock :source="source" />
```

## Code Blocks

Always specify a language:

| Language tag | When to use |
|---|---|
| `ts` | TypeScript |
| `vue` | Vue SFC |
| `json` | JSON / JSONC |
| `html` | HTML snippets |
| `scss` | SCSS |
| `markdown` | Markdown examples |

Use fenced blocks (` ``` `) for all multi-line code. Use inline backticks for single-line values, commands, identifiers.

## Tables

- Always include a header row and a separator row
- Align separator dashes to match column header width (`---`)
- Keep columns readable — wrap long descriptions with natural line breaks if needed

## Links

Use inline link syntax: `[display text](url)`.

- For external URLs, prefer descriptive text over bare URLs
- Reference packages with their full scoped name in backticks: `` `@uis/uisvue` ``

## Lists

- Unordered: `-` bullet (not `*` or `+`)
- Ordered: `1.` for all items (let the renderer handle numbering)
- Indent nested items 3 spaces (aligns with the text of the parent item)
- Keep list items parallel in tense and structure
